title: createProduct has no idempotency guard — any mid-flight failure produces duplicate Whatnot products on retry
impact: When PublishService retries a failed publish (common for transient GraphQL/network errors during the per-variant listing loop), createProduct runs again from scratch and creates a SECOND Whatnot product, because nothing checks whether this canonical product already has a Whatnot product id. The seller accumulates orphaned/duplicate live products, and inventory is split across them.
evidence: adapter.ts:216 `const created = await apiClient.createProduct(...)` with no prior existing-mapping lookup; mapping persistence at adapter.ts:252-268 is after the listing loop and is itself wrapped in a try/catch that only warns (adapter.ts:269-272). createListing throwing at adapter.ts:242 rejects the whole method before any mapping is saved.
fix: Before creating, look up an existing mapping for canonicalProduct/variants and short-circuit to updateProduct if found. Persist the product-level mapping immediately after apiClient.createProduct succeeds (before the listing loop) so a retry can resume rather than recreate; or make the listing loop tolerant/per-variant resumable.
verifier_note: Verified end-to-end. createProduct unconditionally calls apiClient.createProduct (216), then loops per-variant creating listings (228-248); createListing/updateListing throwing (242/246) rejects the whole method BEFORE any mapping is saved (mapping persistence is after the loop at 252-268, and is itself best-effort try/catch). Crucially the caller PublishService routes CREATE vs UPDATE purely on getMappingsByProductIdAndConnection (publish.service.ts:93,109) which queries via ProductVariant->ProductId, NOT PlatformProductId. On a mid-loop failure zero mappings exist, so a retry sees existingMappings.length===0 and calls createProduct again (publish.service.ts:122-130), creating a SECOND live Whatnot product. No idempotency guard, no rollback of the already-created product. Reachable on any transient GraphQL/network error during the listing loop. High confirmed.
corrected_location: whatnot.adapter.ts:216 (createProduct, no pre-check) + :228-248 (listing loop) + :252-268 (mappings only after loop); caller publish.service.ts:109-137

title: Token refresh failure falls through with a stale token instead of refreshing, and a persist failure logs-and-continues — request can succeed with a rotated-but-unsaved token, locking the connection out
impact: A transient Supabase write failure during a token rotation silently strands the connection: the in-flight request works, masking the problem, but the persisted refresh token is dead. The seller's Whatnot sync goes dark and only a manual reconnect fixes it. The code's own comment acknowledges this is CRITICAL yet it does not fail the operation.
evidence: api-client.ts:273-289: persist wrapped in try/catch that only `this.logger.error('CRITICAL: failed to persist rotated refresh token ...')` then control reaches `return newAccessToken;` regardless. Comment at :284 'A rotated-but-unpersisted refresh token strands the connection on restart.'
fix: If persisting the rotated tokens fails, throw (UnauthorizedException/Internal) so the operation fails loudly and the rotation can be retried atomically, rather than returning a token whose refresh counterpart is unsaved. Alternatively persist BEFORE returning and treat persist failure as fatal for this request.
verifier_note: Verified exactly as described. refreshAccessToken obtains a new access+refresh pair (256-271), then persists inside a try/catch that on failure only logs 'CRITICAL: failed to persist rotated refresh token' (283-286) and falls through to `return newAccessToken` (288) regardless. Whatnot rotates refresh tokens (every refresh invalidates the prior one — documented in the file header and the comment at 284). So a transient Supabase write failure during rotation lets the in-flight request succeed while the now-only-valid refresh token is never stored; the dead stored token then locks the connection out on the next refresh cycle, requiring manual reconnect. Real availability/correctness defect. Medium is appropriate: genuinely harmful and self-inflicted lockout, but conditioned on a DB write failing precisely during a token-rotation window (uncommon).

title: Order webhook hard-codes currency 'USD' fallback and coerces totals with Number(...||0), risking silent 0/NaN totals and wrong-currency records
impact: Orders can be recorded with a $0 total or a defaulted USD currency when the payload shape differs from the assumed one, corrupting revenue/reporting data with no warning. Lower severity because it depends on payload-field naming, but Whatnot's schema is partner-gated/beta and field names are not pinned in this code.
evidence: adapter.ts:597 `currency: order.currency ?? order.total?.currency ?? 'USD'`; :599 `total_price: Number(order.total?.amount ?? order.total_price ?? 0)`; :607 `price: Number(item.price?.amount ?? item.price ?? 0)`.
fix: Warn (and consider rejecting/queuing for review) when total_price resolves to 0 from absent fields, and avoid defaulting currency to USD silently — log when currency had to be assumed so mis-shaped payloads are visible.
verifier_note: Verified literally: handleOrderWebhook uses currency: order.currency ?? order.total?.currency ?? 'USD' (598), total_price: Number(order.total?.amount ?? order.total_price ?? 0) (599), and line-item price: Number(item.price?.amount ?? item.price ?? 0) (607), with no validation/warning when the total resolves to 0 from missing fields. If the real Whatnot payload names the amount differently, the order persists with a $0 total and a defaulted USD currency silently. It does not crash and depends on a payload-shape mismatch that isn't demonstrated here, so severity is genuinely low — but the described silent $0/assumed-USD data-quality risk is real for a partner-gated/beta schema whose field names aren't pinned. Confirmed at low.

title: createProduct partial failure leaves orphaned live Depop listings with no mapping (no rollback)
impact: Durable orphaned inventory on Depop: live listings selling with no link back to Anorha inventory, so a sale on an orphan listing never decrements canonical stock (oversell risk) and the seller cannot manage it from the app.
evidence: Loop at depop.adapter.ts:231 `const result = await this.depopApiClient.upsertProduct(connection, payload.sku, payload.body);` runs for every payload BEFORE the mapping loop at line 246 `for (const payload of payloads) { ... createMappingWithIdentifiers(...) }`. upsertProduct (depop-api-client.service.ts:103) throws on non-2xx. A throw on payload[2] skips the entire mapping block; payloads[0..1] are already live on Depop with no mapping row.
fix: Create the mapping immediately after each successful upsertProduct inside the SAME loop iteration (move createMappingWithIdentifiers into the create loop), so every live listing has a mapping the instant it exists; or wrap the loop in a try/catch that records partial successes and surfaces them (returning created ids) instead of discarding them on throw.
verifier_note: Verified directly. createProduct (depop.adapter.ts:231-237) calls upsertProduct for EVERY payload in a first loop before any mapping is written; the mapping loop is at 246-259, AFTER the upsert loop completes. upsertProduct (depop-api-client.service.ts:104-114) throws on non-2xx (or on a 2xx body missing product_id, line 108-110), and the throw propagates straight out of createProduct because the mapping loop has not started yet. So for a multi-variant canonical product, if variant N fails, variants 1..N-1 are already live on Depop with NO mapping row. I confirmed the caller PublishService.catch (publish.service.ts:162-200) does NOT roll back / delete the already-created listings; it just returns success:false. The orphan is real and reachable: Depop is a fashion resale platform and the adapter maps each size/variant to its own SKU-keyed listing (header comment), so multi-payload products are a normal apparel case, not an edge case. Consequences are genuine: an orphan live listing has no link back to canonical inventory, so a sale on it never decrements canonical stock (oversell), it cannot be updated/delisted from the app, and a subsequent syncFromPlatform/persistDepopProducts would re-ingest it as a NEW canonical product (duplicate). The only self-healing is a user-initiated re-publish re-PUTting the same deterministic SKUs idempotently and then mapping them; there is no automatic retry inside PublishService (verified: no retry logic there), so the orphan window is at minimum until manual re-publish and permanent if the canonical set changes. High severity stands (data/inventory/money on a reachable path). Minor finder inaccuracy: PublishService does not auto-retry, but that does not change the orphan outcome.

title: updateProduct never deletes variants removed in Anorha; advertised deletedVariantIds reconciliation is a no-op
impact: Variants deleted in Anorha remain purchasable on Depop indefinitely. A buyer can purchase a withdrawn item; the resulting order may not map cleanly back, and stock the seller believes is gone keeps selling — a silent inventory-integrity divergence.
evidence: depop.adapter.ts:281 `deletedVariantIds: [] as string[],` is declared but the only mutations in the loop (lines 299-314) push to updatedVariantIds/createdVariantIds. No call to deleteProduct/deleteVariant or mapping diff exists anywhere in updateProduct.
fix: Before/after the upsert loop, fetch existing mappings for the product (getMappingsByProductIdAndConnection or by the set of SKUs), compute the set present in mappings but absent from the incoming canonicalVariants, call depopApiClient.deleteProduct(connection, staleSku) for each, mark the mapping disabled, and push the ids into deletedVariantIds.
verifier_note: The behavioral claim is accurate: updateProduct (depop.adapter.ts:292-320) only iterates the payloads built from the CURRENT canonicalVariants and upserts each; deletedVariantIds is initialized to [] at line 281 and never appended to. There is no diff of existing mappings against the incoming variant set and no deleteProduct/deleteVariant call, so a variant removed in Anorha and re-published leaves its Depop listing live. I also confirmed no separate reconciliation/delist of removed variants exists in publish.service.ts, and the getSyncLogic shouldDelist(qty<=0) path would NOT catch a removed variant because no inventory level is pushed for it at all. HOWEVER, the finder's framing that the 'advertised deletedVariantIds reconciliation is a no-op' overstates it: deletedVariantIds is part of a SHARED return-type contract across all adapters, and Square (square.adapter.ts:620) carries the same unfilled field with an explicit 'Not handled in this simplified version' comment, while Clover/Whatnot do fill it. More importantly, I grepped every consumer of the updateProduct result and NO caller reads deletedVariantIds to drive behavior, so it is a reported-only field, not a wired reconciliation step that is silently broken. The real-world harm (withdrawn variant stays purchasable) is genuine but conditional on a user deleting a variant then re-publishing rather than deleting the product, and matches an accepted simplification elsewhere in the codebase. Downgrading medium to low.

title: Push uses connection currency, discarding the per-listing currency captured on import
impact: A Depop shop trading in GBP/EUR whose connection currency is missing or wrong will have listings (re)published with a USD price amount, mispricing the item in the wrong currency on a money-bearing field.
evidence: depop.adapter.ts:60 `return connection.PlatformSpecificData?.currency || 'USD';` and depop.mapper.ts:173-176 builds `price: { amount: formatPriceString(price ?? 0), currency }` using only the passed-in currency; the stored Metadata.depopCurrency from import (mapper line 101) is never read on push.
fix: In mapCanonicalToDepopPushPayloads prefer variant.Metadata.depopCurrency when present, falling back to the connection currency, so round-tripped listings keep their original currency.
verifier_note: Verified. On import the mapper writes the listing's real currency into variant Metadata.depopCurrency (depop.mapper.ts:101). On push, mapCanonicalToDepopPushPayloads builds price.currency solely from the `currency` parameter (depop.mapper.ts:175); it reads variant.Metadata.depopBrand (182) and depopCondition (184) but NEVER reads depopCurrency back out. The adapter sources that currency arg from connectionCurrency = connection.PlatformSpecificData?.currency || 'USD' (depop.adapter.ts:59-61), passed at 223 (createProduct), 289 (updateProduct), and 358 (createVariant). So a non-USD shop whose connection currency is unset or wrong will have listings (re)published with a USD currency on the price field while the stored per-listing currency is discarded. This is a real money-bearing-field correctness issue. Severity correctly low: it only manifests when connection.PlatformSpecificData.currency is missing/incorrect (Depop shops are single-currency, so a correctly-configured connection currency would normally match), and the finder notes the inventory-only RMW path is unaffected. Real but conditional and self-limited to misconfigured connections; low stands.

title: CSV import via the connection-save fallback is a total no-op: submitImport early-returns on connectionId 'csv-import'
impact: A real seller doing a CSV import — whenever the pseudo-connection insert fails (missing OrgId, RLS, transient DB error, or the documented prior 'UserPlatforms' failures) — loses the entire import with zero feedback. They tap Confirm, see a brief spinner, and nothing happens. No data is imported, ever.
evidence: CSVColumnMappingScreen.tsx:191-198 navigates with `connectionId: 'csv-import'`; useImportSession.ts:964 `if (!connectionId || connectionId === 'csv-import') return;` is the first line of submitImport. No alternate CSV submit path exists (grep across MappingReviewScreen/MatchDeck/useImportSession found none that sends importedProducts to a commit/import endpoint).
fix: Either (a) block the fallback: if the connection insert fails, surface an error and do not navigate into a flow that can't commit; or (b) give submitImport a real CSV path that POSTs importedProducts (originalData) to a CSV bulk-import endpoint when isCSVImport is true, instead of early-returning. At minimum, don't render a 'Complete Import' CTA for connectionId === 'csv-import' that resolves to a no-op.
verifier_note: Control flow verified. submitImport (useImportSession.ts:964) begins `if (!connectionId || connectionId === 'csv-import') return;` and fetchMappingSuggestions does the same at :306, BEFORE the isCSVImport handling at :307. So when CSVColumnMappingScreen's insertError fallback (CSVColumnMappingScreen.tsx:192-198) navigates with connectionId:'csv-import', no suggestions load and DoneOverlay->handleConfirmMapping (MappingReviewScreen.tsx:230-237) -> submitImport returns instantly: no commit POST, no PublishConfirmation nav, no AsyncStorage record. The overlay is even dismissed (setDoneVisible(false) at :233 runs after the no-op await), leaving the user with nothing. This is a real silent dead-end. DOWNGRADE critical->high because it is NOT the primary path: the happy path inserts into the real PlatformConnections table (CSVColumnMappingScreen.tsx:174-186) and gets a real UUID, which passes the guards and DOES commit. The fallback only fires on an actual insert failure (RLS/missing OrgId/transient). The finding's 'documented prior UserPlatforms failures / always failed' rationale is stale — the current code inserts into PlatformConnections, not the nonexistent UserPlatforms table — so the deadend is an error-path bug, not an every-import loss.

title: CSV-mapped fields (quantity, barcode, description, brand, condition, cost, compareAtPrice) are silently dropped at commit
impact: Direct data loss on every successful CSV import: stock quantity (inventory!), barcodes, cost (margin), and product details the seller explicitly mapped are thrown away. The seller believes they imported a full catalog; they imported title+sku+price+image only.
evidence: useImportSession.ts:321 stores `originalData: p`; grep for originalData shows it is read NOWHERE in the commit path. commitItems (1044-1068) builds platformProduct from s.platformProduct only — which for CSV holds only sku/title/price/imageUrl (308-316). quantity/barcode/description never appear in the POST body at 1071-1075.
fix: When isCSVImport, carry the full originalData (or an explicit field set) into commitItems' payload (e.g. a `csvFields`/`attributes` object) so the backend can persist quantity/barcode/description/etc., and ensure the backend create-new path reads them.
verifier_note: Verified end to end. CSVColumnMappingScreen builds transformedData carrying all 15 canonical fields (CANONICAL_FIELDS at :31-47, transform at :158-166). fetchMappingSuggestions (useImportSession.ts:308-322) lifts ONLY sku/title/price/imageUrl into platformProduct and stores the full row in originalData (:321). grep confirms originalData is read NOWHERE in any commit path (only the type def importSession.ts:79 and the write at :321). commitItems (:1044-1068) builds platformProduct exclusively from s.platformProduct.{id,sku,title,price,imageUrl,parentId} and never touches originalData, so on a successful CSV import quantity/barcode/description/brand/category/condition/size/color/weight/cost/compareAtPrice are all discarded before the POST at :1071-1075. Genuine data loss (inventory quantity, cost/margin, barcodes) on the working CSV path. Severity high stands.

title: CSV price with thousands separators or currency symbols parses to 0 (silent money loss)
impact: A seller importing a CSV where prices are formatted with commas or symbols gets items priced at $0.00 with no warning, which (if pushed to platforms or used for sync) sells inventory for free or corrupts pricing.
evidence: useImportSession.ts:315 `price: Number(p.price) || 0,`. Same naive coercion is the kind of bug the project already fixed for eBay query prices per memory; CSV input is even more user-formatted.
fix: Strip non-numeric characters before parsing: `const cleaned = String(p.price ?? '').replace(/[^0-9.\-]/g, ''); const price = cleaned ? Number(cleaned) : 0;` and ideally surface a warning row for prices that fail to parse rather than defaulting to 0.
verifier_note: Verified. Line 314 is `price: Number(p.price) || 0`. Number('1,299.99') and Number('$50') are NaN, and `NaN || 0` is 0. Spreadsheet/marketplace CSV exports commonly include thousands separators and currency symbols. Price is one of the four fields that DO survive to commit (commitItems sends platformProduct.price at :1049), so a comma/symbol-formatted price reaches the backend as 0. Real silent money/pricing corruption on the working CSV path. Medium is appropriate (CSV-only, and only when the source formats prices with separators/symbols).

title: submitImport posts an empty items array with no guard — re-surfaces the empty-payload commit failure
impact: All-unmatched / all-ignored imports either 500 (user sees a raw 'Failed to confirm mappings: <errText>' alert) or silently complete with 0 imported, with no clear 'nothing to import' empty state. Confusing dead-end for a legitimate edge case.
evidence: useImportSession.ts:1034-1035 filter; 1071-1075 unconditional POST; MappingReviewScreen.tsx:132-139 opens DoneOverlay on remaining===0 regardless of selection; 413-handleConfirmMapping → submitImport.
fix: Before POSTing, if commitItems.length === 0 and draftLog has no actionable answers, short-circuit to a clear 'Nothing to import' state (and skip the network call) instead of posting an empty body.
verifier_note: Verified. commitItems filters to `isSelected && action !== 'IGNORE' && action !== 'UNMATCHED'` (:1034-1035); when every item is UNMATCHED/IGNORE this is []. submitImport then POSTs `{ items: [], decisions: draftLog, syncRules }` with no client-side empty guard (:1071-1075). DoneOverlay opens whenever remaining===0 (MappingReviewScreen.tsx:132-138, remaining = reviewDeckCases(...).length) regardless of how many items are selected, and its Confirm calls submitImport via handleConfirmMapping (:230-237). reviewDeckCases (classifyMatch.ts:321-330) excludes anorha_to_platform and orphan cases, so an all-UNMATCHED / all-anorha_to_platform set yields remaining 0 and an empty commit. committedCount then falls back to commitItems.length===0, so the completion screen reads 'Imported 0 items', or the backend 500s per project history. Reachable edge case, confusing dead-end, but no money/data/tenant harm. Medium stands.

title: Import progress banner spins forever for any backend status outside the hardcoded terminal set
impact: A returning user can be greeted by a permanently-stuck 'Importing…' banner that never resolves and cannot be cleared except by the manual ✕, on every launch, even though the import actually finished or errored. Erodes trust in import state.
evidence: useImportProgress.ts:12 TERMINAL set; :64-77 only clears on TERMINAL.has(status); no timeout on stored.startedAt. ImportProgressBanner.tsx:31 renders 'Importing…' whenever active.
fix: Broaden terminal detection (treat any status not in a known active set as terminal, or include common terminal aliases), and add a stale-guard: if stored.startedAt is older than N minutes, clear it. Optionally clear when processed >= total.
verifier_note: Code matches the claim: TERMINAL = {'completed','completed_with_errors','failed'} (:12); isTerminal = TERMINAL.has(status) (:65); the stored AsyncStorage record is cleared ONLY when isTerminal (:74-76); otherwise it reschedules poll (:83-85) and the persisted record survives app restarts (poll re-reads LAST_IMPORT_STORAGE_KEY at :46). There is no max-age / give-up on stored.startedAt, and ImportProgressBanner renders 'Importing…' whenever active (ImportProgressBanner.tsx:31-32). So IF the backend ever reports a terminal status outside the hardcoded three (e.g. 'success'/'cancelled'/'done'), the banner spins forever on every launch. DOWNGRADE medium->low: the failure is fully contingent on a backend status vocabulary I cannot verify from this repo (the operation endpoint lives in the backend), and a manual ✕ dismiss exists (:43-45, dismiss->clear removes the record). It is a real latent robustness gap, not a confirmed live break.

title: Unguarded importDraft.completed.length can turn a successful commit into a false 'Import Error' alert
impact: Edge case (only when committedCount is 0/falsy AND the draft shape is partial), but the failure mode is bad: the import committed server-side, yet the user is told it failed and may retry, risking a duplicate import.
evidence: useImportSession.ts:1102 access; :585-588 sets importDraft = await draftRes.json() with no shape validation; the type ImportDraft requires completed/summary but the runtime payload is not guaranteed to.
fix: Use full optional chaining: `(importDraft?.completed?.length || 0) + (importDraft?.summary?.autoResolved || 0)`.
verifier_note: Verified. Line 1102 is `itemsTotal: committedCount || (importDraft?.completed.length || 0) + (importDraft?.summary.autoResolved || 0)`. The optional chain stops at importDraft, so if importDraft is a truthy-but-partial object lacking completed/summary, `.completed.length` / `.summary.autoResolved` throws a TypeError. importDraft is set at :586-588 by `(await draftRes.json()) as ImportDraft` — a compile-time cast with zero runtime shape validation; the ImportDraft type (importSession.ts:194-204) requires completed/summary but the payload is not guaranteed to. This runs inside submitImport's try after a successful commit POST, so a server-side success would be caught and shown as a false 'Import Error', risking a duplicate retry. Reachability is narrow: only when committedCount is 0/falsy (so the && rhs is evaluated) AND the draft GET returned a partial shape. Low stands.

title: Debounced draft-save PUTs confirmedMatches: [] for a zero-item session
impact: Mostly log noise and a potential server-side draft clobber on empty input; not user-visible directly because the catch is silent.
evidence: useImportSession.ts:752-767 builds confirmedMatches from suggestions unconditionally; :595 sets suggestions to [] on fetch error, which would trigger this effect with an empty payload.
fix: Skip the PUT when suggestions.length === 0 (or when the array was set as an error fallback), so an empty draft is never written.
verifier_note: Verified. The debounced autosave effect (:748-773) guards `!connectionId || !Array.isArray(suggestions) || isCSVImport` (:751). `Array.isArray([])` is true, so an empty array (NOT null) passes the guard and PUTs `{ confirmedMatches: [] }` (:752-767). suggestions is set to [] on fetch error (:595), which re-triggers this effect with an empty payload. CSV is excluded (isCSVImport guard), and the null state is excluded, so this only fires for non-CSV zero-item/error sessions. Impact is bounded: errors are swallowed in the try/catch (:768-770) so it is not user-visible; worst case is server log noise / a potential backend draft clobber on empty input per project history. Low stands.

title: SyncRulesScreen overwrites SyncRules with a shape the sync engine doesn't understand → user's directives silently ignored
impact: Seller's explicit sync directives (direction, source-of-truth, propagation) are silently discarded; pull-only/manual selections do not stop outbound writes, so Anorha can overwrite a live store's listings/inventory the user intended to leave untouched. Money/inventory integrity risk.
evidence: SyncRulesScreen.tsx:118-134 builds {syncDirection,sourceOfTruth,autoCreate,autoUpdate,syncInventory,syncPricing,mode,schedule,destinations} and full-overwrites SyncRules. sync-rules.service.ts:13-26 defines productDetailsSoT/inventorySoT/propagateChanges/allowPushToPlatform etc. sync-event-listeners.service.ts:432 `if (syncRules.propagateChanges === false) return false;` else defaults to propagate=true at 440-442.
fix: Stop writing SyncRules directly from the client. POST to the canonical PUT /sync-rules/:connectionId (sync-rules.controller.ts) with the backend field names, or have the screen merge into (not replace) the existing object and emit propagateChanges/propagateCreates/propagateUpdates/allowPushToPlatform/allowPullFromPlatform/productDetailsSoT/inventorySoT. Map UI direction → backend enum (see SYNC-002).
verifier_note: Verified against actual code. saveSyncRules does a raw supabase.from('PlatformConnections').update({ SyncRules: {syncDirection, sourceOfTruth, autoCreate, autoUpdate, syncInventory, syncPricing, mode, schedule, destinations, updatedAt} }) — a full column overwrite written directly to Supabase, bypassing the backend SyncRulesService.updateSyncRules/applyDirectionPreset entirely. The engine reads a DIFFERENT key set: I confirmed grep shows ZERO backend consumers of the mobile keys sourceOfTruth/autoCreate/autoUpdate, while the live gates read propagateChanges/propagateUpdates/propagateCreates (sync-event-listeners shouldPropagateToConnection, reachable from the @OnEvent product-change listener at line 50-54) and allowPushToPlatform (sync-coordinator outbound-push guards). Because the mobile write never sets those flags AND never runs applyDirectionPreset, after a save they are all absent. The gates default-open: propagateUpdates !== false -> true and allowPushToPlatform === false -> false (so push proceeds). Net: a user selecting 'pull-only' still has Anorha pushing product/inventory changes OUT to the live store. Reachable, authenticated, money/inventory-integrity harm. Confirmed high.
corrected_location: /Users/dosagie/Documents/CodeProjects/sssync_mobile_test/src/screens/SyncRulesScreen.tsx:115-151 (saveSyncRules) and :85-113 (loadSyncRules); enforced by sssync-bknd sync-coordinator.service.ts:600,1544,1886,2430 (allowPushToPlatform gate) + sync-event-listeners.service.ts:432-443 (propagate* gate)

title: syncDirection / sourceOfTruth enum values don't match backend; canonical-configured connections show no selection and round-trip to garbage
impact: The sync-rules UI misrepresents the actual configuration (wrong/blank direction, always 'Anorha' source even when the platform is authoritative) and persists values the engine cannot act on, compounding SYNC-001.
evidence: SyncRulesScreen.tsx:16 `type SyncDirection = 'two-way' | 'push-only' | 'pull-only'`; line 101 `setSyncDirection(rules.syncDirection || 'two-way')`. Backend sync-rules.service.ts:24 `syncDirection?: 'bidirectional' | 'pull_only' | 'push_only'`; :256 throws BadRequest on anything else; SoT fields are productDetailsSoT/inventorySoT 'PLATFORM'|'ANORHA' (:13-14).
fix: Adopt the backend enums in the screen (bidirectional/pull_only/push_only and productDetailsSoT/inventorySoT PLATFORM/ANORHA), or translate at the boundary in both loadSyncRules and saveSyncRules. Drive all saves through the canonical sync-rules endpoint so applyDirectionPreset runs.
verifier_note: Verified. Mobile declares SyncDirection 'two-way'|'push-only'|'pull-only' (line 16) and SourceOfTruth 'sssync'|'platform' (line 17). Backend canonical enum is 'bidirectional'|'pull_only'|'push_only' (sync-rules.service.ts:24/255-257) and source-of-truth lives in productDetailsSoT/inventorySoT 'PLATFORM'|'ANORHA' (:13-14). (a) loadSyncRules:101 setSyncDirection(rules.syncDirection || 'two-way') — a backend-configured connection has syncDirection:'bidirectional', which matches none of the 3 radio options, so no direction renders selected; rules.sourceOfTruth is never present (backend uses productDetailsSoT/inventorySoT) so it always falls back to 'sssync' regardless of real SoT. (b) On save the hyphenated/lowercase values are written verbatim and are unreadable by the engine. One correction to the finder's evidence: the cited validateSyncRules BadRequest throw (:256) is NOT reachable on the mobile save path because the write goes straight to Supabase, not through the backend validator — so it does not 'throw', it silently persists garbage. The misattribution does not change the verdict; the UI-misrepresentation + engine-unreadable harm is real. Effectively a sub-facet of SYNC-001. Confirmed high.

title: SyncRules 'Disconnect' hard-DELETEs the connection and culls inventory while telling the user it was merely 'disabled'
impact: A seller who opens Sync Settings and taps 'Disconnect <Platform>' expecting a reversible pause instead permanently deletes the connection and triggers full inventory cleanup (archived products, deleted variants). Silent, irreversible data loss with misleading copy.
evidence: SyncRulesScreen.tsx:162-167 `fetch(.../api/platform-connections/${connectionId}, { method: 'DELETE' })` then `Alert.alert('Disconnected', '${platformName} connection disabled.')`. Backend controller :63-72 routes DELETE→deleteConnection; service :579 `cleanupOrphanedInventory(connection, userId, 'full_cleanup')`. ConnectionsScreen.tsx:118-122 uses POST `/disconnect` with `cleanupStrategy: 'keep'`.
fix: Make the SyncRules disconnect use POST /{id}/disconnect with cleanupStrategy:'keep' (match ConnectionsScreen) and, if a hard delete is ever intended, gate it behind an explicit destructive confirmation that names the consequence. At minimum fix the copy to match the actual action.
verifier_note: Verified end to end. SyncRulesScreen.disconnectPlatform fires fetch DELETE /api/platform-connections/{id} then Alert.alert('Disconnected', '<platform> connection disabled.'). The controller (guarded by SupabaseAuthGuard, normal reachable authenticated path) routes @Delete(':id') -> deleteConnection, whose comment explicitly says 'Use actual delete instead of soft-disable'. deleteConnection calls cleanupOrphanedInventory(connection, userId, 'full_cleanup') BEFORE supabase.delete() — full_cleanup archives fully-orphaned products (line 730-732) and DELETES orphaned variants from mixed products (line 735-739), then the PlatformConnection row is permanently removed (:586-595). By contrast ConnectionsScreen.disconnectPlatform (ConnectionsScreen.tsx:118-122) POSTs /disconnect with cleanupStrategy:'keep', and disconnectConnection only runs cleanup when strategy==='soft_delete' (service:558), so 'keep' preserves all data and never deletes the row. Two screens, two opposite destructive semantics; the destructive one is mislabeled 'disabled'. Silent irreversible data loss with misleading copy. Confirmed high.
corrected_location: /Users/dosagie/Documents/CodeProjects/sssync_mobile_test/src/screens/SyncRulesScreen.tsx:157-172 (disconnectPlatform); backend platform-connections.controller.ts:63-73 (DELETE->deleteConnection) + platform-connections.service.ts:569-598 (full_cleanup then row delete) + :694-743 (archive products / delete orphaned variants)

title: OAuth connect treats a callback with no status param as success (false-positive 'connected')
impact: User can be shown 'connected' for a platform that never actually authorized; the connection silently isn't there, and the inventory scan never starts. Confusing failure with no error surface.
evidence: usePlatformConnect.ts:109-117: `const { status, connectionId } = parseCallback(result.url); if (status === 'error') return {success:false,...}; ... return { success: true, connectionId };` — null status falls through to success. Bare redirect built at :81-83 has no query params.
fix: Require an affirmative signal before declaring success: treat success only when status==='success' OR a connectionId is present; otherwise return a soft failure ('connection did not complete'). Keep the existing error branch.
verifier_note: Verified. connect() returns { success: true, connectionId } whenever result.type==='success' && status !== 'error' — the comment at :113 ('status "success" or absent -> treat as connected') confirms a null status is treated as success. parseCallback returns status:null when the deep link carries no query string, which is exactly the design for 'bare' redirect platforms (Shopify/Facebook -> anorhaapp://auth-callback, built at :80-83 with no params). I confirmed the backend buildRedirectUrl (auth.controller.ts:27-96) always stamps status=success|error on both the URL-parse and web-path branches, so the normal happy path is fine. But any return to the app that does NOT pass through buildRedirectUrl (a malformed/stale/replayed callback, or a provider/native deep-link to the bare scheme without a status param) is reported as a successful connection while no PlatformConnection exists and startScan is skipped (no connectionId). Real false-positive-success on an off-happy-path callback; user sees 'connected' with no connection and no error surface. The provider-deny case usually routes through the backend callback first (which stamps status=error), so the reachable trigger is narrower than 'any cancel' — medium, not high, matching the finder's own rating.

title: Realtime subscription falls back to an unfiltered channel when the internal user id can't be resolved
impact: Under a transient auth/me-view failure the app subscribes far wider than intended: extra refetch churn and a single point of failure (RLS) for not surfacing other tenants' activity timing. Not a confirmed data leak, but a defense-in-depth gap and a wasted-work path.
evidence: platformConnectionsRealtime.ts:30-34 sets currentUserId=null on error; :43-48 `...(currentUserId ? { filter: ... } : {})` → no filter when null.
fix: If currentUserId can't be resolved, do NOT subscribe (or retry resolution) rather than subscribing unfiltered; treat a null id as a setup failure that schedules a retry like CHANNEL_ERROR does.
verifier_note: Verified. getUserLike() resolves the internal UUID inside a try/catch; on any throw currentUserId=null (:32-34). The postgres_changes options spread ...(currentUserId ? { filter: `UserId=eq.${currentUserId}` } : {}) so when null the UserId filter is omitted (:47) and the channel listens to ALL PlatformConnections changes, firing onChange()->caller refetch on every other tenant's connection change. The code's own comment (and Supabase realtime semantics) note RLS still scopes the broadcast row server-side, so this is not a confirmed row-content leak — only change-signal/refetch churn and a single point of failure on RLS for not surfacing other tenants' activity timing. Real defense-in-depth + wasted-work gap, only under a transient auth/me-view failure. The finder rated it low and that is correct.
corrected_location: /Users/dosagie/Documents/CodeProjects/sssync_mobile_test/src/lib/platformConnectionsRealtime.ts:29-34 (currentUserId=null on throw) and :41-48 (filter omitted when null)

title: Price-only edit in QuickProductDetailSheet silently zeroes that location's inventory on save
impact: Direct inventory data loss: a routine price-only edit on the barcode/quick-edit sheet resets the location's quantity to 0, which then syncs to the connected sales channel — overselling protection lost, real stock figures destroyed, with no warning to the user.
evidence: QuickProductDetailSheet.tsx:67 `useState<...>({})`; :320 `[key]: { ...prev[key], price }`; :334 `quantity: data.quantity || 0`; :335 `price: data.price`. AddProductScreen.tsx:4700 `quantity: u.quantity` → PUT body `{ updates: payloadUpdates }` (:4707-4714). VariantInventoryEditor.tsx:83 emits price-only update.
fix: Do not default a missing quantity to 0 on save. Either seed inventoryUpdates with the current per-location quantity when the sheet opens, or in handleSave omit `quantity` from the payload entirely when the user never edited it (send only the fields actually changed) and have the API treat absent fields as 'leave unchanged'. Same for AddProductScreen payload construction.
verifier_note: Verified end-to-end. inventoryUpdates starts as {} (line 67) and both price handlers merge only the changed field: handlePriceChange does { ...prev[key], price } (319-321) and the inline handleUpdateInventory does {...(prev[key]||{}), [field]:value} (687-693). VariantInventoryEditor emits field-scoped 'price' updates (lines 83/134). For a price-only edit inventoryUpdates[key] therefore has no quantity. handleSave coerces quantity: data.quantity || 0 (334) and AddProductScreen sends quantity: u.quantity (4700) to PUT /api/products/:variantId/inventory. I read the backend handler: it calls inventoryLedger.setAbsolute({ target: update.quantity, reason:'manual_set' }) (1471-1479), and setAbsolute passes p_target straight to rpc_set_inventory_absolute (ledger service 282-292) — an authoritative absolute set, so target 0 zeroes the location. It then emits inventory.sync (1505) to propagate cross-platform. Real inventory data loss on a routine price-only quick-edit. Critical confirmed.
corrected_location: QuickProductDetailSheet.tsx:316-322 + :684-694 (field-scoped merge) -> :334 (quantity: data.quantity || 0); AddProductScreen.tsx:4700; backend sssync-bknd/src/products/products.controller.ts:1471 inventoryLedger.setAbsolute({ target: update.quantity })

title: Publish-readiness hardcodes SKU and ignores the registry's requiredFields — blocks valid listings, passes incomplete ones
impact: Sellers are blocked from publishing legitimate listings on channels that don't use SKUs, and are allowed to publish listings missing fields the channel actually requires (description/images), risking downstream platform rejection after the user believes they're done.
evidence: platformRequirements.ts:56-62 (`hasSku = ...; return hasTitle && hasSku && hasPrice && ...`), :75-77 (always pushes 'sku' when blank). config/platforms.ts:198 facebook requiredFields has no 'sku'; :133/:154/:231/:246 square/clover/whatnot/depop = ['title','price']. The registry-derived DEFAULT_REQUIREMENTS (platformRequirements.ts:6-8) is never read by isPlatformReady.
fix: Drive isPlatformReady/getMissingPlatformFields from getPlatformRequirements()[platformKey] (the registry requiredFields) instead of the hardcoded title/sku/price list, mapping each required field key (price→hasPlatformPrice, images→non-empty array, category→id present, etc.). Remove the unconditional SKU check.
verifier_note: Verified. isPlatformReady hardcodes hasTitle && hasSku && hasPrice && (!categoryRequired || hasCategory) (62) and never reads DEFAULT_REQUIREMENTS (built at 6-8 but only exposed via the unused getPlatformRequirements). getMissingPlatformFields always pushes 'sku' when blank (75-77) and only checks title/sku/price/category. The registry (config/platforms.ts) confirms square/clover/whatnot/depop requiredFields = ['title','price'] (133/154/231/246) and facebook = ['title','price','description','images'] (198) — none require sku; shopify/ebay/facebook/amazon require description+images which are never validated. readyPlatforms (GenerateDetailsScreen 1277-1281) uses isPlatformReady and canPublish derives from it (1284); SKU defaults to '' (507) so a blank-SKU Facebook/Square listing is reachable and reported not-ready/missing-SKU, and an image/description-less shopify/ebay listing is reported ready. Both halves real. High (not critical: canPublish only fully blocks when NO platform is ready, and backend may re-validate at publish).

title: Inventory list shows the primary platform's stock even when filtered to a different platform/location
impact: Misleading stock figures while filtered: a seller reviewing Square-only inventory sees Shopify quantities, can misjudge what's in stock per channel/location, and the low-stock/out-of-stock badges are wrong for the filtered context.
evidence: InventoryOrdersScreen.tsx:894 `getPrimaryPlatformInventory = (variantId)` (no platform/location arg); :921 `platformPriority = [...listPlatforms(...).map(d=>d.key),'pool']`; :922-926 returns first priority platform with qty>0; :1089/:1099/:1114 callers; InventoryListCard.tsx:261-285 renders totalQuantity.
fix: When selectedPlatformType/selectedLocationIds are active, compute totalQuantity from only the matching platform's (and selected locations') InventoryLevel rows instead of the fixed priority winner; pass the active filter context into getPrimaryPlatformInventory.
verifier_note: Verified. getPrimaryPlatformInventory(variantId) (894) has no platform/location parameter and returns the first platform in priority order [connectable platforms..., 'pool'] (921-926) with qty>0. The platform filter (971-1011) and location filter (1027-1063) only filter WHICH variant ids display; totalQuantity is computed from getPrimaryPlatformInventory at 1089/1099/1114 with no awareness of selectedPlatformType/selectedLocationIds. InventoryListCard renders totalQuantity as '{n} Units Left' (282) and drives low/out-of-stock badge styling (isLowStock/isOutOfStock). So filtering to Square still shows Shopify (primary) stock and wrong badges. Misleading display, no data loss — medium correct. (Showing primary-platform stock in the unfiltered All view is by-design to avoid double-counting; the defect is specifically that the number ignores the active filter.)
corrected_location: InventoryOrdersScreen.tsx:894-937 (getPrimaryPlatformInventory takes only variantId) used at :1089/:1099/:1114; InventoryListCard.tsx:282

title: Malformed price (multiple decimals) reaches the inventory PUT as NaN→null from the quick-edit path
impact: A fat-fingered price like '19.9.9' is silently lost (serialized to null) on the quick-edit save rather than corrected or rejected, so the intended price change never lands and the seller isn't told.
evidence: VariantInventoryRow.tsx:80 `text.replace(/[^0-9.]/g,'')`; :84 `Number(num || '0')`. QuickProductDetailSheet.tsx:335 `price: data.price` (no parseFloat/Finite guard). AddProductScreen.tsx:4701 `price: u.price`; :4713 `JSON.stringify({ updates: payloadUpdates })`. Contrast ListingEditorForm.tsx:3569 `if (field === 'price') value = toPrice(value)`.
fix: Sanitize the price at input (collapse to a single decimal) and again before save (parseFloat + Number.isFinite, default/abort on NaN). Easiest: apply the same toPrice() guard in QuickProductDetailSheet.handleSave / handlePriceChange that ListingEditorForm already uses.
verifier_note: Mechanism verified: price inputs strip with /[^0-9.]/g (permits '1.2.3'), then Number(num||'0') yields NaN (VariantInventoryRow 80/84, editor 80/83 and 131/134). QuickProductDetailSheet stores it verbatim (319) and handleSave passes price: data.price unsanitized (335); AddProductScreen forwards price: u.price (4701) into JSON.stringify (4713), and NaN serializes to null. So a malformed price is silently dropped — accurate. BUT I read the backend PUT handler: its DTO (products.controller.ts:1357-1364) has no price field and the handler never reads price, so price is ignored on this path for ALL inputs, valid or NaN — the 'intended price change never lands' impact is dominated by the backend dropping price, not by the NaN. As a frontend input-validation gap (multiple-decimals -> NaN with no toPrice guard, unlike ListingEditorForm) it is real but an edge case with redundant impact. Downgraded critical-of-money framing to low.
corrected_location: VariantInventoryRow.tsx:79-86 / VariantInventoryEditor.tsx:79-84,130-135 (replace permits multiple dots); QuickProductDetailSheet.tsx:335; AddProductScreen.tsx:4701,4713

title: Quick-edit save reports success per item but never reconciles partial backend failures
impact: On a partial failure the seller sees one generic error yet some edits did persist; the UI and server diverge and a naive retry re-applies the already-saved updates. Combined with the quantity-zeroing bug this can re-zero stock.
evidence: AddProductScreen.tsx:4688 `for (const [variantId, variantUpdates] of ...)`; :4716 `if (!response.ok) throw`; :4726 single catch → Alert. QuickProductDetailSheet.tsx:345 `await onSave(updates)`; :349-351 generic catch, no state reconcile.
fix: Collect per-variant results, surface which succeeded/failed, and only clear inventoryUpdates entries that the backend confirmed; on partial failure show which items still need saving rather than a blanket error.
verifier_note: Verified. onSave loops per variant (4688) and throws on the first non-ok response (4716-4718); earlier variants in the loop have already been PUT successfully. The single catch shows a blanket 'Failed to save updates' (4728) with no per-item status or rollback, and QuickProductDetailSheet's catch shows a generic error (351) without clearing inventoryUpdates, so optimistic values persist while the server is partially applied; a naive retry re-applies already-saved updates. The QuickProductDetailSheet can hold multiple (option) variants so the multi-PUT partial-failure path is reachable. Real but only on the uncommon multi-variant partial-failure case, and the consequence is UI/server divergence + non-idempotent retry rather than guaranteed loss. Low confirmed.
corrected_location: AddProductScreen.tsx:4688-4719 (per-variant loop, throw on first non-ok at 4716) + :4726-4729 generic catch; QuickProductDetailSheet.tsx:345-351 (no state reconcile)

title: Mobile reads canonical variant image as .ImageUrl but backend sends PrimaryImageUrl — every matched thumbnail silently lost
impact: Every item matched via the scan path (matchType SKU/BARCODE) shows NO canonical/inventory thumbnail in the import review UI — the user comparing 'is this the same as something I already have?' sees a blank image for the existing item, undermining the core same-or-not decision. Pure silent data loss; no error surfaces.
evidence: Mobile useImportSession.ts:458 `imageUrl: item.suggestedCanonicalVariant.ImageUrl,` and :535 `imageUrl: item.suggestedCanonicalVariant.ImageUrl,`. Backend initial-sync.service.ts:246 maps `imageUrl: (v as any).PrimaryImageUrl ?? null` and mapping.service builds suggestedCanonicalVariant as a Partial<ProductVariant> (mapping.service.ts:353 `suggestedCanonicalVariant: match ?? null`) — the row only has `PrimaryImageUrl`. Contract import.contract.ts:83-92 lists `PrimaryImageUrl`, never `ImageUrl`.
fix: In useImportSession.ts:458 and :535 read `item.suggestedCanonicalVariant.PrimaryImageUrl` (optionally `?? item.suggestedCanonicalVariant.ImageUrl` for safety). Note the sibling reads at :492/:502 correctly fall back `Sku||sku` etc., so only the image field is wrong here.
verifier_note: Verified directly. useImportSession.ts:458 and :535 both read item.suggestedCanonicalVariant.ImageUrl. The backend builds suggestedCanonicalVariant as `match ?? null` (mapping.service.ts:353) where `match` is a Partial<ProductVariant> raw DB row; the ProductVariant image column is PrimaryImageUrl everywhere in the backend (toCanonicalRef line 726 reads .PrimaryImageUrl; cross-org services confirm the column name), and the contract row object (import.contract.ts:88-91) types only PrimaryImageUrl, never ImageUrl. So .ImageUrl is always undefined and the scan-derived canonical thumbnail is silently dropped. Note the backend ALSO sends a correct suggestedCanonicalProduct (mapping.service.ts:354 via toCanonicalRef, with a proper imageUrl), but mobile ignores it and reconstructs from the raw variant with the wrong key — the data exists, only the UI mapping is wrong. Real but UI-only: it degrades the same-or-not visual comparison; it does not corrupt data, money, tenant isolation, or the committed mapping. High is overstated; medium is correct.

title: Import matchType enum mismatch: mobile has AI_SEMANTIC and lacks MANUAL; backend has MANUAL not AI_SEMANTIC
impact: Display/typing only — no zod parse runs on this UI type, so a server-sent 'MANUAL' flows through as an unexpected string and any client switch keyed on matchType falls to its default branch (e.g. a manual match could render as 'no match'/unlabeled). 'AI_SEMANTIC' is dead. Low because it cannot crash and the field is non-load-bearing for commit.
evidence: Mobile importSession.ts:75 `matchType?: 'BARCODE' | 'SKU' | 'TITLE' | 'NONE' | 'AI_SEMANTIC';`. Backend anorha-import.dto.ts:19-25 ANORHA_MATCH_TYPES includes 'MANUAL', not 'AI_SEMANTIC'.
fix: Align importSession.ts:75 to the contract: `'BARCODE' | 'SKU' | 'TITLE' | 'NONE' | 'MANUAL'` (drop AI_SEMANTIC, add MANUAL), or better, import ImportMatchType from src/contracts/import.contract.ts.
verifier_note: Verified directly. Mobile UI type importSession.ts:75 declares matchType including 'AI_SEMANTIC' and omitting 'MANUAL'. Backend ANORHA_MATCH_TYPES (anorha-import.dto.ts:19-25) is ['BARCODE','SKU','TITLE','NONE','MANUAL'] and the synced contract MATCH_TYPES (import.contract.ts:19) matches it exactly — 'AI_SEMANTIC' does not exist server-side, 'MANUAL' is absent client-side. Confirmed downstream impact and confirmed harmlessness: classifyMatch.ts matchWhy() switch (line 397) has a dead 'AI_SEMANTIC' case (line 404) and NO 'MANUAL' case, so a server-sent MANUAL match falls to default (line 406-407) and renders the generic 'similar listing' evidence string instead of an accurate manual-match label. No zod parse runs on this UI type, so it cannot crash and the field is non-load-bearing for commit. Cosmetic mislabel of the on-demand 'why matched?' line only. Low is correct.
